questions.question: What does a parent component receive when it holds a template ref to a `<script setup>` child that has NOT called `defineExpose`?
options: The full component instance, identical to the Options API behavior
An empty object `{}` because `<script setup>` components are closed by default
A Proxy that throws when any property is accessed
The component's DOM root element instead of the instance

questions.question: What is a common use case for `defineExpose` in a `BaseInput` wrapper component?
options: To make the input's `v-model` binding available to grandparent components
To expose imperative methods like `focus()` and `blur()` so parents can call them via a template ref
To allow the parent to override the input's scoped CSS styles
To broadcast input events to sibling components without using a store

questions.question: When should you prefer `defineExpose` over props and emit for parent-child communication?
options: Whenever performance requires avoiding reactive data flow
For genuinely imperative actions (like `reset()` or `focus()`) that don't fit a declarative prop/emit pattern
When the child component has more than five props to pass back
When the parent and child are in different route views
